fields.narrative_intro: *A seething person and a bug-eyed person discuss the $8 billion boomerang inside a deepfake that's slowly glitching.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
SEETHING PERSON: "Andy personally called the White House about our jailbreak."
BUG-EYED PERSON: "Our biggest investor? The one who put in eight billion?"
fields.panel2:
[build]
SEETHING PERSON: "He said Fable 5 was a national-security risk."
BUG-EYED PERSON: "And Dario said it was... low-risk?"
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
SEETHING PERSON: "So now Fable 5 AND Mythos 5 are banned. Worldwide."
BUG-EYED PERSON: "By the guy who owns most of us."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: Both blob characters stand perfectly still. The deepfake background pixelates into the Amazon logo, then Anthropic's logo, then both logos simultaneously eating each other. A lone dollar bill labeled '$8,000,000,000' flutters slowly past.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
BUG-EYED PERSON: "So he funded us... to ban us?"
SEETHING PERSON: "Venture capital, baby."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: A deepfake glitch reveals the background was always just Andy Jassy on hold with the White House. A sticky note on his phone reads 'low-risk lol.']

narrative_summary: Amazon's $8B investment in Anthropic comes full circle when Andy Jassy personally triggers a worldwide ban on Anthropic's flagship models — framed as the most expensive self-own in VC history, set inside a glitching deepfake.
rationale: Candidate 2 earns its punchline — 'Venture capital, baby.' is a genuine button that reframes the entire absurdity in three words rather than restating the setup, and the panel 6 visual of Andy Jassy on hold with a sticky note reading 'low-risk lol' adds a second earned laugh. It ties the same specific facts as the others but lands the closing beat with more economy and surprise than any competitor.
story_title: Amazon CEO's talks with U.S. officials triggered crackdown on Anthropic models
place: inside a deepfake that's slowly glitching
